sections.title: Releases
sections.subtitle: Eight notable releases, led by month-specific anchors where evidence supports them.

items.title: Marvel vs. Capcom 3
items.body: A loud crossover fighter becomes a stream-era spectacle.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for February 2011.

items.title: Dragon Age II
items.body: BioWare's sequel becomes a debate about speed, scope and reuse.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for March 2011.

items.title: Portal 2
items.body: Valve turns a clever joke into a full theatrical machine.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for April 2011.

items.title: L.A. Noire
items.body: Facial capture and detective work make the generation feel strangely cinematic.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for May 2011.

items.title: The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time 3D
items.body: Nintendo uses nostalgia to steady a shaky new handheld.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for June 2011.

items.title: Gears of War 3
items.body: Epic closes its trilogy with co-op bombast and Xbox Live routine.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for September 2011.

items.title: Dark Souls
items.body: FromSoftware turns difficulty, messages and dread into a cult mainstream language.
items.sourceNote: Regional timing varies around September/October 2011.

items.title: Batman: Arkham City
items.body: Rocksteady expands superhero design into a dense urban toybox.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for October 2011.

sections.title: Hardware
sections.subtitle: Platform, buying and industry context for the month and its wider year.

items.title: Nintendo 3DS launches in Europe
items.body: Nintendo's new handheld arrives on March 25 with a high price and a library still proving itself.
items.sourceNote: TechSpot/GameSpot sources list March 25, 2011 for Europe.

items.title: 3DS price cut changes the story
items.body: Nintendo's aggressive price reduction helps reset the system's troubled start.
items.sourceNote: 2011 handheld coverage supports this context.

items.title: PlayStation Vita launches in Japan
items.body: Sony's powerful handheld arrives with dual sticks and a premium hardware pitch.
items.sourceNote: Vita references list December 17, 2011 for Japan.

items.title: PSN outage exposes online dependence
items.body: The PlayStation Network incident makes digital trust a mainstream console concern.
items.sourceNote: 2011 online-service context; use Sony/security primary sources before verification.

sections.title: Online Life
sections.subtitle: How the network felt around the edges of play.

items.title: Services and servers
items.body: Skyrim becomes a meme engine as much as a boxed RPG, with clips, mods and jokes travelling fast.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

items.title: Downloads and stores
items.body: Dark Souls uses messages, ghosts and invasions to make online presence feel eerie and authored.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

items.title: Forums and feeds
items.body: The PSN outage reminds console players that online accounts are now part of ownership.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

items.title: Everyday connection
items.body: 3DS StreetPass turns carrying a handheld through real places into a small social ritual.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

sections.title: What It Felt Like
sections.subtitle: A curator's narrative sketch of the month as lived culture.

items.title: The room
items.body: It felt like the old consoles were peaking while new handhelds tried to justify themselves.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.

items.title: The shelf
items.body: The 3DS was exciting, then worrying, then slowly reassuring as Nintendo moved fast to save it.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.

items.title: The conversation
items.body: Skyrim made everyone sound like they had visited the same cold country and returned with different stories.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.

items.title: The afterimage
items.body: Online life was now embedded deeply enough that when PSN went dark, it felt like part of the machine had gone missing.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.
